scenario.name: boots_into_streaming_mode7_overworld
scenario.description: Under RANDOM power-on the ROM boots ($7E:E000=SFDB) into a static top-down Mode 7 overworld streamed from the large authored 512x512 world (several windows wide AND tall). The initial 128x128 seed window centred on the spawn (258,258) renders REAL authored terrain: the screen lower-left (60,180) is the GRASS meadow floor (a green, BGR midpoint of the dark/light grass checker — tolerance spans both checker shades since the fine 8x8 texture flips dark<->light with sub-pixel scroll). A screenshot blob (>=4 distinct colours) proves a real TEXTURED world rendered (grass + road + mountain + forest), not a flat fill or a black band. ELNORA — the purple-robed, staff-wielding heroine (OAM 0, a true 16x16 OBJ, size bit clear) — boots IDLE facing DOWN (front view = base tile 16, attribute 0x20 = priority 2, no flip) screen-centred at (120,104). Reads rendered framebuffer pixels + OAM bytes, not a proxy.

scenario.name: avatar_walks_east_world_streams_new_content
scenario.description: Continuing from boot, holding RIGHT walks Elnora EAST across several tiles; the Mode 7 CAMERA scrolls under the screen-centred heroine and NEW world content streams into the VRAM window (no pop-in / black band). Elnora TURNS to face the direction she walks: after the east walk she is in her RIGHT profile = base tile 20 (attribute 0x20 = priority 2, no flip — LEFT would be this same tile 20 H-flipped to attribute 0x60). She stays screen-centred at (120,104) — the world moves, not the sprite (camera-follows-player). A screenshot blob (>=4 distinct colours) confirms a real streamed world still renders after walking past the seed window. Reads OAM bytes + rendered framebuffer, not a proxy.

scenario.name: avatar_blocked_by_mountain_wall
scenario.description: The two spawn axes are open authored road corridors (so Elnora can traverse several windows each way); collision is exercised by stepping OFF a corridor into a BLOCKED mountain range. Elnora is STOPPED by tile collision — she cannot walk into the mountain. Facing follows her last d-pad press, so after the east-then-NORTH drive she is in her UP (back) facing = base tile 18 (attribute 0x20 = priority 2, no flip). She stays screen-centred at (120,104) (the camera carries world position; the sprite never moves), and the world still renders (blob >=4 distinct colours) with the mountain wall on screen. Collision is LUT-derived from the flat tilemap byte (tile_terrain_lut) — there is no separate collision table. Collision correctness against the world position is asserted byte-exact in tests/test_mode7_explore.py (MX-010, walks a few tiles east off the spawn column then north into the wall, reads the camera world tile from WRAM).
